stories.summary: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

stories.headline: When Suzuki met Suzuki: why a Tokyo dating agency is matching couples with the same name
stories.summary: Japan’s ban on married couples having different surnames has prompted an event to highlight people’s reluctance to change their nameAt the very least, the three men and three women calming their nerves on a Friday evening at a venue in Tokyo know they have one thing in common.Spaced out across booths, they will soon be placed in pairs and given 15 minutes to get to know one another. stories.headline: Barito Renewables Hits Two-Year Low on Shareholding Concerns
stories.summary: Shares of PT Barito Renewables Energy tumbled Monday to their lowest in more than two years after the Indonesian stock exchange flagged the company for having a highly concentrated shareholder base.

stories.headline: Iraq Tells Buyers to Collect Crude as Its Oil Can Cross Hormuz
stories.summary: Iraq has told Asian traders and refiners they can load its crude as vessels carrying the country’s oil are now able to transit the Strait of Hormuz thanks to an Iranian exemption, testing buyers’ confidence in the security guarantee.

stories.headline: Rupee Extends Rally After Biggest Surge in 12 Years on RBI Moves
stories.summary: The Indian rupee climbed, extending its biggest rally in 12 years after the central bank doubled down on curbing speculation against the local currency.

€500 billion-worth European data economy troubles continue
stories.summary: As AI grabs the spotlight, Europe’s data economy still powers billions in value, yet fragmented GDPR enforcement continues to hold innovators back. The Digital Omnibus was meant to help, but the EU now risks retreating from even modest reform.

stories.headline: Inside Armenia’s underground wellbeing retreat where asthma patients seek relief
stories.summary: Once state-funded, the centre now faces possible closure after the government withdrew support, saying the treatment lacks scientific evidence.

stories.headline: Trump Revels in Threats to Commit War Crimes in Iran
stories.summary: The president said he would bomb Iran “back to the Stone Ages.” Until this administration, American leaders had insisted they were trying to follow international law in war.

stories.headline: US Rescues Missing Airman as Iran Strikes Gulf Arab States
stories.summary: US forces rescued an airman from Iran more than a day after his fighter jet was shot down and President Donald Trump threatened to target the country’s infrastructure, while the Islamic Republic’s continued attacks damaged Kuwait’s oil headquarters and shut down an Emirati petrochemicals plant.

stories.headline: Trump threatens Iran with ‘Hell’ over Strait of Hormuz in profane post
stories.summary: Trump escalated threats against Iran’s power plants, bridges and other infrastructure in an expletive-laden post on Truth Social on Easter morning.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy warns US-Iran war could divert critical aid from Ukraine
stories.summary: President Zelenskyy has warned that a prolonged US-Israeli war with Iran is threatening the supply of vital Patriot missiles and boosting Russia’s economy through surging oil prices.

stories.headline: US pilot rescued as Trump threatens to hit Iran infrastructure in profanity-laden social media post
stories.summary: A US military pilot has been found in Iran after a search-and-rescue mission. Soon after, US President Donald Trump issued a threat to Iranian infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed in a profanity-laden social media post.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Syria to cooperate on security, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: During a trip to Damascus, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said the two countries agreed to work together.

stories.headline: Israeli air strike hits crowd in Gaza City
stories.summary: Several Palestinians were rushed to hospital with severe injuries after an Israeli air strike hit a crowd in Gaza City.

stories.headline: Israel continues deadly attacks on Gaza while striking Iran, Lebanon
stories.summary: Israel has kept up its attacks on Gaza while striking Iran and Lebanon, killing at least four Palestinians on Sunday.

stories.headline: In first Easter blessing as pontiff, Pope Leo XIV urges those who can unleash wars to 'choose peace'
stories.summary: In his first Easter blessing, Pope Leo XIV urged "those who have the power to unleash wars" to "choose peace" as Christians around the world celebrate Easter Sunday.

stories.summary: Ministry clarifies clause affecting those up to age 45 that is part of legislation that came into effect in JanuaryA little-noticed clause in sweeping changes to Germany’s military service policy has caused uproar after it emerged that the law requires men aged up to 45 to get permission from the armed forces before any significant stay abroad, even in peacetime.The legislation, which went into effect on 1 January, aims to bolster the military an

stories.headline: Cambodia unveils statue to honour famous landmine-sniffing rat
stories.summary: The statue honours the late African giant pouched rat who sniffed out more than 100 landmines during his lifetime.

stories.headline: How China fell for a lobster: What an AI assistant tells us about Beijing's ambition
stories.summary: The AI agent sparked a frenzy of "raising lobsters" in March, with users training the tool to suit their needs.

stories.summary: Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund is advancing a plan to combine the asset management units of state-owned lenders in order to boost their regional competitiveness.

stories.headline: Indian Bank Stocks’ $95 Billion Rout May Deepen, Analysts Say
stories.summary: India’s bank stocks are likely to face further losses as the central bank tightens its grip on rupee trading and rising energy prices weigh on profit outlooks, analysts say.

stories.summary: The architect of President Trump’s mass deportation campaign wants “a moratorium on immigration from third world countries until we can heal ourselves as a nation.” The chaos in Minneapolis has not pushed him off that course.

stories.headline: Some Voters Say Congress Is Too Old. These Black Democrats Aren’t Leaving.
stories.summary: As older members of Congress head for the exits amid growing pressure for fresh faces in the Democratic Party, some of the most seasoned Black lawmakers are resisting retirement.

stories.headline: In Hungarian election, Trump and Putin are backing Viktor Orban
stories.summary: Viktor Orban, who has built strong ties to the MAGA movement and the Kremlin, faces a tough electoral challenge from center-right candidate Peter Magyar on April 12.

stories.headline: Labour to back down on foie gras and fur bans to ease EU trade deal
stories.summary: Exclusive: Animal welfare charities ‘bitterly disappointed’ UK government plans to backtrack on manifesto promisesThis article contains an image of a duck being force-fed that some readers may find upsettingThe UK government is to break a manifesto commitment to ban foie gras imports, and has declined to stop fur imports, after the EU made these red lines in its discussions for a trade deal.Animal welfare charities say they are “bitterly disappoi

stories.headline: UK police arrest seven protesters near RAF base used by US
stories.summary: The activists were protesting the alleged use of the RAF base as a departure point for US aircraft.

stories.headline: State medical insurance reform aims to expand access to healthcare
stories.summary: The reform will introduce digital referrals, a national health insurance fund and a guaranteed package of medical services funded through the state budget. Officials say the changes aim to improve efficiency, expand access and reduce informal payments.

stories.headline: Explosives found near gas pipeline connecting Serbia and Hungary, Vučić says
stories.summary: Serbian president Aleksandar Vučić has said that explosives were found by police near a pipeline that carries gas from Russia to Serbia and Hungary, but did not elaborate on potential suspects or their motives.

stories.headline: Help or hinderance? Amateur archaeologist digs at German castle ruins without permission
stories.summary: Germany's cultural heritage community is embroiled in a row after an amateur archaeologist uncovered an ancient construction. The problem however is that he didn't have permission to dig at the site of Metilstein Castle near Eisenach.

stories.headline: Leipzig Eierlei festival celebrates the 'eggstraordinary' art of Easter decorations
stories.summary: For some painting eggs is an integral part of Easter, but exhibitors at the Eierlei Festival in Leipzig take this tradition to a new level as craft and art experts show what can be done with supposedly "simple" decorations.

stories.headline: Trump Pledged a Quick End to the Iran War, but He Hasn’t Explained How

stories.summary: Videos have targeted Viktor Orbán's election rival, who could unseat him after 16 years in office.

stories.headline: New Attorney General, Same Albatross: Trump’s Quest for Retribution
stories.summary: The name atop the Justice Department’s organizational chart matters less than the presence of a president whose demands for revenge have become so extreme that even his most obsequious appointees have fallen short.

stories.headline: A Meat Plant Closed in Nebraska. Then Politics Became a Focus for These Latinos
stories.summary: Politically disconnected young men, especially Latinos, helped fuel President Trump’s victory, but in this economy, they could move to unconventional candidates like Dan Osborn in Nebraska.

stories.summary: Physicians from 39 countries are being pushed out of U.S. hospitals as a policy blocks their ability to work.

stories.headline: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia
stories.summary: Quake with epicentre west-north-west of Ternate island shakes cities and prompts regional tsunami warningOne person has been killed after a 7.4 magnitude earthquake struck Indonesia’s Ternate island, damaging buildings and triggering small tsunami waves.The quake, which had a depth of 35km, occurred on Thursday at 6.48am local time, according to the United States Geological Survey. Its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-north-west of Ternate, an

stories.headline: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one
stories.summary: The quake struck between the islands of Manado and Ternate. Tsunami warnings have now been lifted.

stories.headline: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits

stories.headline: Macron calls Trump's remarks on his marriage 'inelegant'
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has said Donald Trump's comments about his marriage were "neither elegant nor up to standard".

stories.headline: Alleged Bondi gunman loses court bid to suppress names of his family
stories.summary: Lawyers for Naveed Akram had argued his mother, brother and sister live in "constant fear".

stories.headline: New Zealand signs defence pact with Cook Islands after quarrel over China deal
stories.summary: Agreement comes after Wellington halted millions in aid to its former colony after Cook Islands formed strategic partnership with Beijing  New Zealand and the Cook Islands have signed a defence and security declaration, ending a year-long diplomatic row that erupted after the Cook Islands struck strategic agreements with China.The Cook Islands was a dependent New Zealand colony from 1901-65 but has since operated as a self-governing nation in “fr

stories.headline: China is trying to play peacemaker in the Iran war - will it work?
stories.summary: It's a turnaround for Beijing, whose official response has been muted so far. Why is China stepping in now?

stories.headline: Asia's migrant workers debate if Gulf jobs are worth deadly risk of Iran war
stories.summary: Iran's strikes on Gulf states have been especially hard for migrants who have long supported these economies to lift their families back home from poverty.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: Abnormally strong jet stream triggers deluge in Middle East, while north Africa braces for 60-80mph gustsAn unusual weather pattern unleashed severe thunderstorms across parts of the Middle East last week, battering countries including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia.
